rt2-4-1.title: Hands-on: Sort a relevant indication from lift-off noise
rt2-4-1.intro: The indication ladder applies to every NDT method, not just film. Scan the probe down the lane and mark where a signal peaks, then make the interpreter's call: which recording was relevant — produced by a real crack — and which was lift-off noise the technique put on screen.

tasks.label: Scan the lane and mark where the indication peaks
tasks.hint: A real flaw kicks the dot off at a steep phase angle; lift-off drifts flat along the horizontal.

tasks.label: Pick the signal a real discontinuity produced
tasks.hint: Relevant means made by the part: choose the steep-phase kick, not the flat lift-off wiggle.

rt2-4-2.title: Hands-on: Orientation decides what you detect
rt2-4-2.intro: Planar discontinuities only show when the interrogating energy crosses their plane — the same rule that hides laminations from a straight RT beam. Rotate the energized yoke so its flux crosses each surface crack near-perpendicular, the way you would hunt service fatigue cracks growing in different directions.

tasks.label: Dust the plate and indicate crack A
tasks.hint: Particles gather only where flux crosses a crack close to 90 degrees — A already lies across the flux.

tasks.label: Re-aim the yoke until crack B indicates
tasks.hint: No single yoke angle holds all three cracks; swing the axis until the flux runs perpendicular to B.

tasks.label: Find the angle that reveals crack C
tasks.hint: C lies in yet another plane — the flux that found B runs nearly parallel to C, so keep rotating.
